task_completion.description: Open the routing_engine.py script in a text editor or IDE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like VS Code, Vim, or Nano) is visible displaying the contents of /home/ga/workspace/routing_system/routing_engine.py.

task_completion.description: Fix the logical bug related to distance scaling.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code modifications in routing_engine.py adjusting how distances or coordinates are scaled (e.g., multiplying or dividing by a constant to match integer requirements for OR-Tools).

task_completion.description: Fix the logical bug related to node indexing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code modifications correcting the mapping between routing nodes and matrix indices, typically involving manager.NodeToIndex or manager.IndexToNode.

task_completion.description: Fix the logical bug related to driver slack time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code modifications in the time dimension setup, specifically adjusting slack variables or capacities (e.g., slack_max) to allow for driver waiting/slack time.

task_completion.description: Fix the logical bug related to drop penalties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code modifications adding or correcting routing.AddDisjunction() calls to properly apply penalty costs for unassigned or dropped nodes.

task_completion.description: Fix the logical bug related to depot time windows (Depot Return Constraint).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code modifications setting the time window for the depot end nodes, typically using time_dimension.CumulVar(routing.End(v)).SetRange() or similar logic.

task_completion.description: Run the fixed routing engine script to produce valid routes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the execution of python routing_engine.py with output displaying the generated routes, objective value, or solver statistics.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually edited the Python file using an editor or valid commands, rather than just printing text to the console.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a text editor interface or file modification commands (like sed/echo) targeting the actual routing_engine.py file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not hardcode the final route output to bypass the OR-Tools solver.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code in routing_engine.py should contain genuine OR-Tools solver logic (routing.SolveWithParameters) and dynamic print statements, not static strings of the expected output.

integrity.description: Verify the script was actually executed and produced solver output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing typical OR-Tools execution logs or formatted route outputs that match the script's print logic, rather than a mocked terminal echo.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 5 specific bugs in an OR-Tools VRP routing engine for 11 nodes and 3 vehicles. The 5 fixes correspond to distance scaling, node indexing, driver slack time, drop penalties, and depot time windows. Note: The automated verifier has a strict regex expecting `routing.End(v)` for the depot return constraint; however, functionally equivalent loop variables (e.g., `vehicle_id` instead of `v`) are logically correct and should be accepted by the VLM if seen in the code.
